operator: TSUBAME Computing Services, Center for Information Infrastructure, Institute of Science Tokyo
architecture: x86_64 (AMD EPYC 9654 Genoa) + NVIDIA H100 GPUs

node.cpu: AMD EPYC 9654 (96 cores / 192 threads, 2.4 GHz) x 2 sockets = 192 cores/node
node.gpu: 4x NVIDIA H100 SXM5 94GB HBM2e per node
node.local_ssd: 1.92 TB NVMe U.2 SSD per node

notes: TSUBAME4 is a GPU-first system: every one of the 240 compute nodes carries 4x NVIDIA H100. The default and typical job takes whole GPU nodes (resource type node_f).

Jobs are sized by RESOURCE TYPE, not nodes/cores: request '-l <resource_type>=<N>' (e.g. '-l node_f=2'). Each resource type fixes cores, memory, GPUs, and local scratch. Different resource types cannot be combined in one job.
Every job needs a maximum run time '-l h_rt=HH:MM:SS' (max 24 hours) and a resource type. Both are required.
Jobs are billed in TSUBAME points to a group via the qsub '-g <group>' argument. Check points with 't4-user-info group point -g <group>'.
TRIAL RUN: submitting with NO '-g' runs a free trial job, limited to 2 resource-type units, 3 minutes, priority -5. Ideal for testing that a program launches.
Priority (qsub -p) is -5 (standard, default), -4 (higher), or -3 (highest); -4/-3 consume points at a higher rate. Always a negative number.
GPU compute mode can be set per job with '#$ -v GPU_COMPUTE_MODE=<0|1|2>' on node_f/node_h/node_q/gpu_1 (0 DEFAULT, 1 EXCLUSIVE_PROCESS, 2 PROHIBITED). MIG is used on node_o and gpu_h.
Software is provided through Environment Modules (module avail/load/purge). Load the same modules in the job script as used to build. With qsub -V, run 'module purge' before 'module load'.
GPU builds: 'module load cuda' and/or 'module load nvhpc'. Intel oneAPI ('module load intel') provides the Intel compilers, MKL, and Intel MPI.

MPI: Intel MPI launches with 'mpiexec.hydra -ppn <ranks/node> -n <total>'; OpenMPI ('module load openmpi/5.0.7-intel') launches with 'mpirun -npernode <ranks/node> -n <total> -x LD_LIBRARY_PATH'. The assigned node list is in $PE_HOSTFILE.
Threaded code: set OMP_NUM_THREADS to the cores per process; hyper-threading is enabled (up to 192 threads/node on node_f).
LD_LIBRARY_PATH and LD_PRELOAD are NOT forwarded by qsub -V (a TSUBAME-specific restriction) — set them inside the job script.
Login nodes are shared: no heavy computation, no parallel/long (>10 min) programs, no ISV/freeware runs, no server processes. Use jobs (qsub) or interactive jobs (qrsh).

queues.normal: Batch jobs via qsub and interactive jobs via qrsh; resource type selectable. The usual path.
queues.interactive: Shared interactive job queue via iqrsh (24 cores, 96 GB, 1 MIG shared by up to 12 users); free for intramural (tgz-edu) / access-card users. For debuggers, visualizers, Jupyter Lab.
queues.subscription: qsub -q prior — compute-node subscription job (waits at most one hour).

modules.intel: Intel oneAPI (ifx/icx/icpx, former ifort/icc/icpc; MKL; loads Intel MPI)
modules.openmpi/5.0.7-intel: Open MPI built with Intel compilers (mpirun)
modules.openmpi/5.0.7-nvhpc: Open MPI built with NVIDIA HPC SDK (CUDA-aware)

compilers.intel_llvm: ifx / icx / icpx; MPI: mpiifx / mpiicx / mpiicpx
compilers.recommended_flags: -xCORE-AVX512 for Intel on EPYC 9654 (Zen4 supports AVX512)

container_runtime: Apptainer (Singularity). Build with 'apptainer build'; run with 'apptainer exec -B /gs -B /apps -B /home --nv <image>'.

storage.home: /home/<g>/<user> — 25 GiB quota, 2M inodes (Lustre, SSD). Check with 't4-user-info disk home'.
storage.work: /work/<g>/<user> — 100 GiB per user; path is $HOME with 'home' replaced by 'work' (${HOME/home/work}). No application needed.
storage.high_speed_group: /gs/fs — high-speed group disk (Lustre, SSD, 372 TB total); purchased per group.
storage.large_scale_group: /gs/bs — large-scale group disk (Lustre, HDD, 44.2 PB total); purchased per group. Check with 't4-user-info disk group -g <group>'.
storage.local_scratch: /local — node-local xfs on NVMe SSD (1.62 TiB usable on node_f), exists only for the job's duration.
storage.quota_command: t4-user-info disk home | t4-user-info disk group -g <group>

accounting.points: t4-user-info group point -g <group> — shows deposit (rate) and balance (remaining TSUBAME points).
accounting.disk: t4-user-info disk home ; t4-user-info disk group -g <group>

network.sinet: 100 Gbps to the internet via SINET6 from the Suzukakedai campus.
network.campus_cifs: Group disks (/gs/fs, /gs/bs) are reachable from on-campus Windows/Mac via CIFS as t4_fs / t4_bs.
